SLP Media← Back to website

Privacy Policy

Last updated: 18 April 2026  |  Version 1.0  |  Governed by UK law

Your privacy matters to us. This policy explains what personal data SLP Media collects, why we collect it, how we use it, who we share it with, and your rights under UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018. We do not sell your data — ever.

1. Who We Are (Data Controller)

SLP Media is the data controller responsible for your personal information on this website.

  • Trading name: SLP Media
  • Contact email: seb.slpmedia@gmail.com
  • Website: slp-media.com

2. What Personal Data We Collect

2.1 Data you provide directly

  • Contact & enquiry data: name, email, phone, company name via contact forms.
  • Qualifying & funnel data: business type, ad budget, revenue, project goals via qualification forms.
  • Booking data: name, email, notes when booking via Calendly.

2.2 Data collected automatically

  • Usage data: pages visited, time spent, browser/device type, referring URL.
  • Technical data: IP address (may be anonymised), approximate location, session identifiers.
  • Cookie data: see our Cookie Policy.

2.3 Data we do NOT collect

We do not collect special category data, payment card details, or children's data. Our services are for business professionals aged 18+.

3. Lawful Basis for Processing

PurposeData usedLawful basis
Responding to enquiriesName, email, phone, companyLegitimate interests — Art. 6(1)(f) UK GDPR
Processing strategy call applicationsName, email, business infoLegitimate interests — Art. 6(1)(b) UK GDPR
Scheduling discovery calls (Calendly)Name, email, booking notesLegitimate interests — Art. 6(1)(b) UK GDPR
Marketing communicationsName, emailLegitimate interests with opt-out — Art. 6(1)(f) UK GDPR & PECR Reg. 22
Analytics & website performanceUsage data, anonymised IPConsent (cookie banner) — Art. 6(1)(a) UK GDPR & PECR
Advertising & retargetingCookie identifiers, usage eventsConsent (cookie banner) — Art. 6(1)(a) UK GDPR & PECR
Legal compliance & record-keepingAny relevant dataLegal obligation — Art. 6(1)(c) UK GDPR

4. How We Use Your Personal Data

  • Respond to enquiries and questions
  • Assess whether our services suit your business
  • Schedule and conduct strategy/discovery calls
  • Send relevant information about our services (opt out any time)
  • Manage ongoing client relationships
  • Improve our website and understand visitor behaviour
  • Comply with legal obligations

We do not use your data for automated decision-making that produces legal or similarly significant effects.

5. How Long We Keep Your Data

CategoryRetention periodReason
Enquiry & contact form data2 years from last contactBusiness records and follow-up
Qualifying funnel / strategy call data2 years from submissionLead management
Calendly booking data2 years (Calendly manages their own retention)Appointment history
Client service data6 years after end of contractLegal and tax obligations
Analytics dataUp to 26 monthsWebsite performance analysis
Cookie consent records1 year or until withdrawnPECR compliance

6. Who We Share Your Data With

Third partyPurposeLocationPrivacy policy
GoHighLevelCRM, email/SMS automationUSA (SCCs apply)View
CalendlyScheduling callsUSA (SCCs apply)View
Meta (Facebook/Instagram)Ad pixel, conversion tracking (consent only)USA (SCCs apply)View
VercelWebsite hostingUSA/EU (SCCs apply)View

We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data. International transfers use IDTA/Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the UK ICO.

7. Cookies and Tracking Technologies

We use cookies with your consent. See our Cookie Policy for full details and to manage your preferences.

8. Data Security

  • HTTPS encryption on all data transmissions
  • Access restricted to authorised personnel only
  • Regular review of data handling practices
  • Reputable, security-audited third-party processors

In the event of a breach likely to affect your rights, we will notify the ICO within 72 hours and you directly where required.

9. Your Rights Under UK GDPR

RightWhat it means
AccessRequest a copy of data we hold about you (Subject Access Request).
RectificationAsk us to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
ErasureAsk us to delete your data where there is no compelling reason to keep it.
RestrictionAsk us to pause processing in certain circumstances.
PortabilityReceive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
ObjectObject to processing based on legitimate interests, including direct marketing.
Withdraw consentWithdraw cookie consent any time via the cookie preference centre.

Contact us at seb.slpmedia@gmail.com to exercise your rights. We will respond within one calendar month.

10. Right to Lodge a Complaint

Information Commissioner's Office (ICO)
Website: ico.org.uk
Helpline: 0303 123 1113
Address: Wycliffe House, Water Lane, Wilmslow, Cheshire SK9 5AF

11. Children's Privacy

Our services are for business professionals aged 18+. We do not knowingly collect data from children.

12. Changes to This Policy

We may update this policy from time to time. The "Last updated" date at the top will always reflect the latest revision.

13. Contact Us

SLP Media
Email: seb.slpmedia@gmail.com
Website: slp-media.com
